rationale: Checked that all 6 Copilot comments from PR #189 were already addressed in commit 05751cb before creating new PR
Changed 'POSIX ERE' to 'GNU sed ERE' in networking-tools post-install.sh because alternation syntax (accept|drop|reject) is a GNU extension, not pure POSIX ERE
Clarified node_exporter filesystem exclusion comment: /sys and /proc are pseudo-filesystems excluded from disk metrics, /dev intentionally left visible for devtmpfs reporting
Clarified that sed without -E uses POSIX BRE (Basic Regular Expressions) where \( \) \| are metacharacters, not ERE
Removed ${#days} -gt 3 string length check as it was redundant with numeric range check against max_days=825
Added max_value parameter 3650 to validate_positive_int call for RENEWAL_DAYS to prevent integer overflow in check_seconds calculation
Added CapabilityBoundingSet= and AmbientCapabilities= to gateway systemd unit to drop all Linux capabilities since gateway uses unprivileged ports (>1024)
Creating new PR #190 instead of reopening PR #189 per user requirement: each iteration must be a new PR on the same branch
Updated validate_positive_int in cert-manager (Dockerfile) to accept optional max_value third parameter matching post-install.sh hooks signature for codebase consistency
Changed get_renewal_days() to use validate_positive_int with max_value parameter instead of separate range check, eliminating code duplication
Updated comment to clarify POSIX ERE syntax vs GNU sed -E flag portability
Changed cut -d'=' -f2 to cut -d'=' -f2- to handle values containing = signs
Removed 2>/dev/null from validate_positive_int call so users see specific validation failure reasons
Improved BRE pattern comment to describe capturing group behavior more accurately
Added comment explaining head -1 behavior for multiple CN values in certificate subject
Added rm -f lockfile after flock subshell to avoid stale lockfiles on disk
Changed health/ready/main handlers to return JSON error responses for API consistency
Simplified README sed pattern by removing trailing context capture that was causing confusion
Added error checking to w.Write() calls in error response paths for consistency with success path error handling
